name: kubelet
explanation: The kubelet integration runs against the Agent's own host node. The shipped `conf.yaml.default` declares `ad_identifiers: [_kubelet]`, which is a built-in Autodiscovery listener — the Agent itself populates the kubelet URL, port, and bearer token via `kubeutil.get_connection_info()`, then `KubeletCredentials` injects the in-cluster ServiceAccount token. None of the per-instance fields are required: the user does not supply a host, port, or credentials. Per pattern O (in-cluster bearer-token auth) plus the agent-managed `_kubelet` listener, this is `generic`. No external network probe is needed because the Agent's runtime auto-detects the local kubelet, so the auto-config method is `other` (host-local detection rather than port scan).
notes: The `_kubelet` AD identifier is a special agent-builtin listener; discovery happens inside the Agent runtime (kubeutil), not via a network probe template.
